metadata.title: Sikkim Travel Seed Data
metadata.scope: seed data for hubs, places, dining, and accommodations across Sikkim
metadata.hub_distribution_note: The user-provided hub percentages total 100.5, so both raw and normalized weights are included.
metadata.data_quality_note: Coordinates and elevations are exact where readily available from public references; smaller or remote hubs use approximate planning centroids. Distances from hubs are practical travel estimates for mock-data generation, not turn-by-turn navigation. Expanded coverage includes curated planning-grade seed entries around real Sikkim hubs and tourism belts so generators can build denser itineraries with nearby food and stay support.

hubs.name: Gangtok
hubs.description: Gangtok is one of the main planning anchors in East Sikkim, and it works especially well as a base for travelers who want smoother routing, broader day-trip options, and a clearer sense of local geography. Sitting around 5410 feet, the hub usually supports stays of about 2 days, although many itineraries stretch longer when travelers want to explore at a slower pace. Its strongest value comes from how easily it connects with Pakyong, Rangpo, and Namchi, which makes it practical for route generation, multi-stop circuits, and realistic overnight planning. The surrounding travel character is shaped by the wider landscape of Gangtok, so this hub can support urban exploration, cultural stops, scenic drives, or countryside detours depending on the trip style.

places.name: Ranka Monastery
places.description: Ranka Monastery is a monastery in East Sikkim that works particularly well from Gangtok, making it a useful stop for itineraries that want stronger local character instead of repetitive sightseeing. It sits roughly 20 km from the hub and around 4500 feet in elevation, so the drive, the air, and the overall mood can feel quite different from lower or more urban parts of the state. The experience is most rewarding for solo, friends, family, and couple, especially when the plan leans into themes like spiritual, cultural, and photography rather than treating the place as a rushed photo halt. Travelers should factor in its low weather sensitivity, the preferred window of winter, Summer, Autumn, and Spring, and the best visiting time around morning when building a realistic schedule.
places.tip: Early morning visits are quieter and better for prayer-hall access.

It sits roughly 11 km from the hub and around 3850 feet in elevation, so the drive, the air, and the overall mood can feel quite different from lower or more urban parts of the state. The experience is most rewarding for solo, friends, family, and couple, especially when the plan leans into themes like roadtrip, landscape, and photography rather than treating the place as a rushed photo halt. Travelers should factor in its medium weather sensitivity, the preferred window of winter, Summer, Autumn, and Spring, and the best visiting time around afternoon when building a realistic schedule.
